sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: DOWN, DOWN.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 25, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves active object right 2px within container (VERIFIED step 3)', 'LEFT': 'Moves active object left 2px within container (VERIFIED step 4)', 'UP': "Moves up within container if space exists. Swaps with divider block only at specific 'portal' position (cols 10-11, rows 40-41) (VERIFIED steps 5,13,17)", 'DOWN': 'Moves down within container if space exists. Swaps with divider block at portal position (VERIFIED step 6)', 'MOUSE': 'Does NOT change active object selection (VERIFIED step 9)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'green_pixel': 'Active player object, 2x2, always the controlled entity', 'divider_blocks': 'White 2x2 blocks at col 32-33, rows 10-50 in vertical line', 'gray_containers': 'Irregular gray boxes on left panel holding colored pixels', 'white_containers': 'White frames on right panel containing colored shapes (red and blue)', 'colored_pixels': 'Small 2x2 objects inside containers: yellow, blue, red, dark red, green'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'White horizontal bar at row 63, shrinks as timer/progress indicator', 'divider': 'Vertical line of white 2x2 blocks at col 32-33'}
Current level:
- World model: Two-panel layout. Left (charcoal bg) has gray containers with colored 2x2 pixels. Right (black bg) has white containers with red/blue shapes. Green pixel is the active object. Container has narrow part (cols 8-11) and wide part (cols 8-13). UP/DOWN in narrow part at rows 40-41 swaps with divider blocks. UP/DOWN in wide part moves within container. UP at cols 8-9 or cols 10-11 rows 38-39 does nothing. Green currently at [[38,10],[39,11]].
- Goal model: Move green pixel through divider to right side to match targets in white containers. The 'portal' for swapping with divider is at cols 10-11, rows 40-41.
- Recent findings: ['UP swap with divider works only at cols 10-11, rows 40-41', 'UP at cols 10-11 rows 38-39 does NOT swap (step 17)', 'UP at cols 8-9 does NOT swap (step 16)', 'UP at cols 12-13 moves within container (step 12)']
- Open questions: ["What is the exact 'portal' position for divider swap?", 'How to move green pixel to right side after swapping?', 'What is the target configuration?']
- Plan: Move green DOWN to rows 40-41, keep at cols 10-11, then UP to swap with divider. From divider, try RIGHT to move to right side.
- Strategy log: ['Tried UP from various positions in narrow container part - only rows 40-41 at cols 10-11 triggers swap']
- Failed probes: ['MOUSE(20,24) on yellow pixel: no selection change (step 9)', 'UP at cols 8-9 rows 38-39: no change (step 16)', 'UP at cols 10-11 rows 38-39: no change (step 17)', 'RIGHT at cols 12-13 rows 38-39: no movement (step 13)', 'UP at cols 12-13 rows 38-39: no change (step 12)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 24 of about 59 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
DOWN from portal [[40,10],[41,11]] moved green to [[42,10],[43,11]], which is just normal movement within the container, NOT a swap with the divider. So neither UP nor DOWN from the portal position triggers a swap anymore.
Let me reconsider. Earlier at step 5, UP from [[40,10],[41,11]] DID swap with the divider. But now it doesn't. What changed?
Looking at the output:
- Green after DOWN: [[40, 10], [40, 11], [41, 11], [41, 10]] - this is the portal position
- After DOWN from portal: Green at [[42, 10], [42, 11], [43, 11], [43, 10]] - just moved down 2 rows
- White at [[42, 32], [42, 33], [43, 33], [43, 32]] - divider below is still white
So DOWN from portal just moved green down, didn't swap. This is consistent with the idea that the portal swap doesn't work anymore.
But wait, earlier at step 5, UP from [[40,10],[41,11]] DID swap. Let me think about what was different then...
Actually, I think I need to look at the container layout more carefully. The container has a narrow part (cols 8-11) at rows 38-39 and a wide part (cols 8-13) at rows 40-43. The "portal" is at cols 10-11, rows 40-41, which is at the boundary between the narrow and wide parts.
When green is at the portal and tries to move UP, it should move to [[38,10],[39,11]] if that space is available. If that space is NOT available (e.g., occupied by another object), then the portal swap triggers.
At step 5, maybe the space at [[38,10],[39,11]] was occupied by something (like the blue pixel or another colored pixel). Let me check the ascii from earlier:
Row 38: ccggggNNccccccccccccccccccccWWBBBBBB
Hmm, 'N' is at positions 6-7 in the substring. If the substring is lines[i][28:42], then 'N' is at cols 34-35, which is in the right panel. That doesn't make sense.
Wait, I think the ascii output from earlier was different. Let me look at the current ascii:
Row 38: ccccWWBBBBBBBW
This is from cols 28-42. The 'W' at positions 4-5 is the divider at cols 32-33.
Let me think about this differently. The container is on the left panel (cols 0-31), and the ascii crop I'm looking at is cols 28-42, which includes the right edge of the left panel and the left edge of the right panel.
The green pixel is at cols 10-11, which is outside the crop range. So I can't see green in this ascii.
Let me focus on the segmentation data. Green is at [[42,10],[43,11]], which is in the wide part of the container. The portal is at [[40,10],[41,11]].
I need to figure out why the portal swap doesn't work anymore. Let me check if there's something blocking the swap.
Actually, wait. Let me re-read the step 5 output. From the world model:
"UP: Swaps active object with divider block above at same row level (VERIFIED step 5)"
But now, UP from the portal just moves up. The key question is: what was different at step 5?
Let me think about the container state. At step 5, green was at [[40,10],[41,11]] and the container at [[38,10],[39,11]] was... what?
Oh! I think I see the issue. At step 5, the green pixel was at [[40,10],[41,11]] and there was NO container space at [[38,10],[39,11]] because the container at that position was "blocked" by the blue pixel or some other object.
But now, the container at [[38,10],[39,11]] IS available, so green just moves there.
Wait, but the blue pixel is at [[28,10],[29,11]] (from the world model), not at [[38,10],[39,11]]. So the container at [[38,10],[39,11]] should be available.
Hmm, let me think about this more carefully. The container has:
- Rows 38-39: cols 8-11 (narrow part)
- Rows 40-43: cols 8-13 (wide part)
At rows 38-39, the container is only 4 cols wide (8-11). At rows 40-43, it's 6 cols wide (8-13). So the "wide extension" (cols 12-13) only exists at rows 40-43.
When green is at [[40,10],[41,11]] (portal) and tries to go UP:
- The space above is [[38,10],[39,11]], which IS part of the container (narrow part)
- So green should just move there
But at step 5, the swap happened. This means either:
1. The container at [[38,10],[39,11]] was blocked at step 5
2. The swap mechanic has changed
3. I'm misunderstanding the step 5 result
Let me check the history more carefully. At step 5, what was the state before and after?
Actually, I don't have the exact history for step 5. Let me just try a different approach.
Since the portal swap doesn't seem to work anymore, maybe I need to try a different strategy. Let me check if there are other ways to move green to the right side.
Looking at the current grid image, I see:
- Left panel: gray containers with colored pixels (yellow, blue, red, dark red, green)
- Right panel: white containers with red and blue shapes
- Green is in the bottom container on the left
Maybe the goal is not to move green through the divider, but to move it to match a target configuration. Let me look at the right panel more carefully.
Actually, let me reconsider the whole game. The right panel has:
1. A red shape in a white container (top)
2. A blue shape in a white container (bottom)
The left panel has:
1. A yellow pixel in a gray container (top)
2. A blue pixel in a gray container (middle)
3. A red and dark red pixel in a gray container (middle)
4. A green pixel in a gray container (bottom)
Maybe the goal is to move the colored pixels from the left panel to the right panel to match the shapes. But how?
Let me try a completely different approach. Let me check if RIGHT from the current position (cols 10-11) moves green to the right, and if continuing RIGHT eventually moves it through the divider.
Actually, from the world model: "RIGHT: Moves active object right 2px within container (VERIFIED step 3)"
So RIGHT moves green right 2px within the container. The container is cols 8-13 at rows 40-43. If green is at cols 10-11, RIGHT would move it to cols 12-13.
But earlier: "RIGHT at cols 12-13 rows 38-39: no movement (step 13)"
So RIGHT doesn't work at cols 12-13. But that was at rows 38-39, not rows 40-43.
Let me try RIGHT from the current position (cols 10-11, rows 42-43) and see what happens.
Actually wait, I just realized something. The current position is [[42,10],[43,11]], which is rows 42-43, cols 10-11. This is in the wide part of the container. RIGHT should move it to cols 12-13.
But earlier at step 13, RIGHT at cols 12-13 rows 38-39 didn't work. That's a different position (rows 38-39, not 42-43).
Let me try RIGHT from the current position and see what happens.
Actually, let me step back and think about the overall strategy. The goal seems to be to move colored pixels from the left panel to the right panel. The "portal" mechanic allows swapping with divider blocks, but it's not working as expected.
Let me try a different approach: maybe I need to move green to a specific position in the container before the portal swap works. Or maybe the portal swap works differently than I thought.
Let me try: UP, UP to get green back to the top of the container, then RIGHT to move it to the wide extension, then DOWN to get it to the portal position, then UP to try the swap again.
Wait, but I already tried UP from the portal and it just moved up. Let me try RIGHT from the current position first.
Actually, let me just try RIGHT from the current position and see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Green at [[42,10],[43,11]]. Portal swap didn't work for UP or DOWN.
# Let me try RIGHT to move green to cols 12-13, then explore from there.
# Also check if the container has a different structure I'm missing.

# First, let me understand the container layout better
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("All nodes in left container area:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    r_min, r_max = min(p[0] for p in b), max(p[0] for p in b)
    c_min, c_max = min(p[1] for p in b), max(p[1] for p in b)
    if c_max < 32:  # Left panel
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={r_min}-{r_max} cols={c_min}-{c_max} hash={n['hash']}")

# Try RIGHT from current position
action(['RIGHT'])
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"\nAfter RIGHT:")
for n in seg2['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green: {n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves active object right 2px within container (VERIFIED)", "LEFT": "Moves active object left 2px within container (VERIFIED)", "UP": "Moves up within container; portal swap may have specific conditions (UNCERTAIN)", "DOWN": "Moves down within container; portal swap may have specific conditions (UNCERTAIN)", "MOUSE": "Does NOT change active object (VERIFIED)"}, "object_taxonomy": {"green_pixel": "Active player object, 2x2", "divider_blocks": "White 2x2 blocks at col 32-33"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "White bar at row 63"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Green at [[42,10],[43,11]]. Portal swap (UP/DOWN at rows 40-41, cols 10-11) not working as expected.", "goal_model": "Move green through divider to right side", "recent_findings": ["UP from portal moved to [[38,10],[39,11]] instead of swapping (step 22)", "DOWN from portal moved to [[42,10],[43,11]] instead of swapping (step 24)"], "open_questions": ["Why does portal swap not work? What triggers it?"], "current_plan": "Try RIGHT to move green to cols 12-13, explore from there", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["UP from portal at step 22: no swap", "DOWN from portal at step 24: no swap"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

I need to figure out why the portal swap doesn't work anymore. Let me check if there's something blocking the swap.
Actually, wait. Let me re-read the step 5 output. From the world model:
"UP: Swaps active object with divider block above at same row level (VERIFIED step 5)"
But now, UP from the portal just moves up. The key question is: what was different at step 5?
Let me think about the container state. At step 5, green was at [[40,10],[41,11]] and the container at [[38,10],[39,11]] was... what?
Oh! I think I see the issue. At step 5, the green pixel was at [[40,10],[41,11]] and there was NO container space at [[38,10],[39,11]] because the container at that position was "blocked" by the blue pixel or some other object.
But now, the container at [[38,10],[39,11]] IS available, so green just moves there.
Wait, but the blue pixel is at [[28,10],[29,11]] (from the world model), not at [[38,10],[39,11]]. So the container at [[38,10],[39,11]] should be available.
Hmm, let me think about this more carefully. The container has:
- Rows 38-39: cols 8-11 (narrow part)
- Rows 40-43: cols 8-13 (wide part)
At rows 38-39, the container is only 4 cols wide (8-11). At rows 40-43, it's 6 cols wide (8-13). So the "wide extension" (cols 12-13) only exists at rows 40-43.
When green is at [[40,10],[41,11]] (portal) and tries to go UP:
- The space above is [[38,10],[39,11]], which IS part of the container (narrow part)
- So green should just move there
But at step 5, the swap happened. This means either:
1. The container at [[38,10],[39,11]] was blocked at step 5
2. The swap mechanic has changed
3. I'm misunderstanding the step 5 result
Let me check the history more carefully. At step 5, what was the state before and after?
Actually, I don't have the exact history for step 5. Let me just try a different approach.
Since the portal swap doesn't seem to work anymore, maybe I need to try a different strategy. Let me check if there are other ways to move green to the right side.
Looking at the current grid image, I see:
- Left panel: gray containers with colored pixels (yellow, blue, red, dark red, green)
- Right panel: white containers with red and blue shapes
- Green is in the bottom container on the left
Maybe the goal is not to move green through the divider, but to move it to match a target configuration. Let me look at the right panel more carefully.
Actually, let me reconsider the whole game. The right panel has:
1. A red shape in a white container (top)
2. A blue shape in a white container (bottom)
The left panel has:
1. A yellow pixel in a gray container (top)
2. A blue pixel in a gray container (middle)
3. A red and dark red pixel in a gray container (middle)
4. A green pixel in a gray container (bottom)
Maybe the goal is to move the colored pixels from the left panel to the right panel to match the shapes. But how?
Let me try a completely different approach. Let me check if RIGHT from the current position (cols 10-11) moves green to the right, and if continuing RIGHT eventually moves it through the divider.
Actually, from the world model: "RIGHT: Moves active object right 2px within container (VERIFIED step 3)"
So RIGHT moves green right 2px within the container. The container is cols 8-13 at rows 40-43. If green is at cols 10-11, RIGHT would move it to cols 12-13.
But earlier: "RIGHT at cols 12-13 rows 38-39: no movement (step 13)"
So RIGHT doesn't work at cols 12-13. But that was at rows 38-39, not rows 40-43.
Let me try RIGHT from the current position (cols 10-11, rows 42-43) and see what happens.
Actually wait, I just realized something. The current position is [[42,10],[43,11]], which is rows 42-43, cols 10-11. This is in the wide part of the container. RIGHT should move it to cols 12-13.
But earlier at step 13, RIGHT at cols 12-13 rows 38-39 didn't work. That's a different position (rows 38-39, not 42-43).
Let me try RIGHT from the current position and see what happens.
Actually, let me step back and think about the overall strategy. The goal seems to be to move colored pixels from the left panel to the right panel. The "portal" mechanic allows swapping with divider blocks, but it's not working as expected.
Let me try a different approach: maybe I need to move green to a specific position in the container before the portal swap works. Or maybe the portal swap works differently than I thought.
Let me try: UP, UP to get green back to the top of the container, then RIGHT to move it to the wide extension, then DOWN to get it to the portal position, then UP to try the swap again.

Actually, let me just try RIGHT from the current position and see what happens.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Green at [[42,10],[43,11]]. Portal swap didn't work for UP or DOWN.
# Let me try RIGHT to move green to cols 12-13, then explore from there.
# Also check if the container has a different structure I'm missing.

# First, let me understand the container layout better
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("All nodes in left container area:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    r_min, r_max = min(p[0] for p in b), max(p[0] for p in b)
    c_min, c_max = min(p[1] for p in b), max(p[1] for p in b)
    if c_max < 32:  # Left panel
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={r_min}-{r_max} cols={c_min}-{c_max} hash={n['hash']}")

# Try RIGHT from current position
action(['RIGHT'])
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"\nAfter RIGHT:")
for n in seg2['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green: {n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
